properties.habitatDescription: Shallow standing-timber flats and shoreline coves on Lake Fork hold the trophy Florida-strain largemouth spawn from late February through April.
properties.source: USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD)

properties.habitatDescription: Old creek channels and the inundated Sabine-headwater bed are the primary channel-catfish habitat in Lake Fork. (Mapped tributary channels: Lake Fork Creek.)
properties.source: USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD)

properties.habitatDescription: TPWD-deployed PVC cubes, artificial habitat, and natural brush clusters in Lake Fork hold crappie year-round. (TPWD sites 1, 2, 4, 5: PVC Cubes/Artificial.)
properties.source: TPWD — Texas Parks & Wildlife Fish Habitat Structures

properties.habitatDescription: Hydrilla mats on the broad northern flats of Sam Rayburn host nationally renowned largemouth spawning.
properties.source: USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD)

properties.habitatDescription: The submerged Angelina River channel through Sam Rayburn is the principal blue catfish corridor. (Mapped tributary channels: Angelina River, Ayish Bayou.)
properties.source: USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD)

properties.habitatDescription: TPWD-deployed PVC cubes and natural brush clusters on Sam Rayburn's main lake hold prolific crappie populations. (TPWD site 1: PVC Cube.)
properties.source: TPWD — Texas Parks & Wildlife Fish Habitat Structures

properties.habitatDescription: Shallow vegetated shoreline pockets and timber-covered flats throughout Toledo Bend support a Top-10 nationally ranked largemouth fishery.
properties.source: USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD)

properties.habitatDescription: The submerged Sabine River channel and feeder-creek mouths are the dominant catfish habitat in Toledo Bend. (Mapped tributary channels: Sabine River, Patroon Bayou, Housen Bayou.)
properties.source: USGS National Hydrography Dataset (NHD)

properties.habitatDescription: TPWD-deployed PVC cubes and natural brush clusters in Toledo Bend's mid-lake creeks hold black and white crappie year-round. (TPWD sites 1, 24: PVC Cubes / 20 PVC Structures; installed 2017/2024.)
properties.source: TPWD — Texas Parks & Wildlife Fish Habitat Structures
